candidates.rationale: Uses iron-based adsorptive media for arsenic removal after oxidation via chemical pre-treatment. Chlorine provides final disinfection and maintains residual in distribution system.
Ion exchange effectively removes arsenic after oxidation converts As(III) to As(V). Chloramine provides longer-lasting disinfection residual compared to free chlorine.
Permanganate oxidizes arsenic species while GAC provides removal through adsorption. UV provides final disinfection without chemical residuals.
Ozone oxidizes arsenic for enhanced removal via coagulation/filtration in conventional treatment. Chlorination provides final disinfection barrier.
Chemical oxidation followed by direct filtration removes arsenic through coagulation and filtration. Chlorine dioxide provides effective disinfection with minimal DBP formation.
